says: Respect the repository maintenance contract: keep bootstrap behavior generic, prefer .agents/repo.json for project-specific knobs, and sync AGENTS.md plus README plus the skill index when commands, hooks, agent assets, integrations, or descriptor rules change.

Reusable bootstrap caches, source mirrors, toolchain fetches, and bootstrap artifacts are accelerators. A miss must not be fatal in normal bootstrap or CI restore paths; only explicit upload/publish/refresh maintenance commands should fail on missing configured inputs. CI caches .local/toolchain plus .local/stamps, not the full .local tree.

Cache the smallest reusable surface. Per-tool specs declare TOOL_PRUNE_PATHS to drop unused subtrees of large toolchains (man pages, unused targets, alternate frontends) after install. CI caches only .local/toolchain plus .local/stamps; never .local/cache or other refetchable payload. Adding a path to the cache requires evidence it is reused across runs and survives a fresh clone deterministically.

Cache writes happen only on non-PR runs (canonical branches). PR jobs restore but never save, to avoid noisy 'another job may be creating this cache' failures from competing immutable-key writers. cache_warm.yml restores daily to defeat the 7-day GitHub LRU; restore misses are not fatal.

If a command both builds and consumes build output, provide a --no-build or equivalent reuse mode. CI runs the explicit build once, then uses reuse-mode test/package/bench/system-test steps to avoid duplicate rebuilds and make failures stage-specific.

Prefer underscores over hyphens for new files, directories, config keys, Python modules, generated identifiers, cache keys, and internal command names. CLI arguments still use conventional - / -- flags. Do not add hyphenated command aliases in this template. Skill folder names under .agents/skills/ are an explicit carve-out and use hyphens.
Skill folder names use hyphens, never underscores. agent_check enforces this. Renaming would break upstream agent runtime skill discovery.

Default communication style in this repo is caveman: terse, no filler, fragments OK, technical content exact. Auto-clarity exception applies for security warnings, irreversible-action confirmations, and multi-step sequences where order risks misread.
Keep .editorconfig as the template editor baseline. Add local VSCode plugins through .agents/repo.json:vscode_extensions and tools/setup rather than hard-coding project-specific extension paths.

Pinned tools live in bootstrap/tools/<tool>.sh which sources either fetch_binary.sh or fetch_source.sh. Helpers do not invent flags; they read declared TOOL_* variables. Every download has a SHA256. Editing a per-tool spec naturally bumps the CI cache key; editing the helpers themselves requires bumping bootstrap/vars/local_cache_key.sh:cache_epoch.

Default license is PolyForm Strict 1.0.0. Owner and year come from .agents/repo.json:product. Permissive licenses (MIT/Apache) would let third parties resell template-derived products without terms; PolyForm Strict was chosen over BUSL because it has no automatic conversion clause.

tools/initialize is idempotent: re-running after a partial failure picks up where it stopped. New steps must remain product-agnostic and re-runnable. Persist any new product knob in .agents/repo.json. Stamp completion at .local/stamps/initialized.

Third-party credentials never live in the repo. Default GitHub token is read from $GITHUB_TOKEN if set else ~/github.token (mode 0600). New integrations must follow the same pattern: env var override, fallback to ~/<service>.token, document the integration in AGENTS.md Integrations and README Integrations.

Append a numbered ADR only when all three are true: hard to reverse, surprising without context, and the result of a real trade-off. Files are NNNN_slug.md with sequential numbering and underscore separators. Update docs/adr/index.md in the same commit.

doc-sync acceptance: AGENTS.md skill table is non-empty and matches .agents/skills/index.md; every skill folder is referenced by at least one of those two surfaces; no row references a skill folder that does not exist; integrations in AGENTS.md mirror README; ADR numbers are sequential with no gaps.
